description: Core pixel-art rules distilled from Slynyrd Pixelblog, Saint11, Pedro Medeiros, AdamCYounis, and Daniel Linssen. Read this BEFORE drawing - it shortcuts most beginner mistakes.

hue_shift.rule: When shading from light to dark, do NOT just decrease lightness. Shift hue toward the cooler/darker neighbor (typically toward purple/blue) AND decrease saturation slightly in mid-tones. When highlighting, shift toward yellow/warm.
hue_shift.saturation_curve: Mid-tones LEAST saturated. Highlights and shadows MORE saturated. NEVER pure gray in mid-tones for tinted objects.
hue_shift.lightness_steps: 3-5 steps for a ramp. Not equal spacing - usually compress mid-tones, expand extremes.

jaggies.rule: Avoid 'jaggies' (stair-step aliasing where line direction changes abruptly). A diagonal line should keep consistent step length: 2-2-2-2 or 3-3-3-3, never 2-3-2-2-3.
jaggies.fix_recipe: If a line goes 2-2-3-2-2, the 3 is the jaggy. Either add a pixel to make it 3-3-3 or remove one to make it 2-2-2.
jaggies.single_pixel_rule: Single isolated pixels jutting out of a curve are ALWAYS wrong. Either continue the run or break to a clean step.
jaggies.source: Pedro Medeiros - Pixel Art Class; Saint11

banding.rule: Banding = parallel bands of two adjacent shades creating an unintentional stripe. Caused by placing each shade exactly along straight diagonals of equal length.
banding.fix_recipe: Vary the band thickness (3px - 2px - 4px - 2px), break with a 1px jut, or insert a 3rd shade to break the parallelism.

anti_aliasing_clusters.rule: Pixel-art AA uses small 'clusters' of intermediate-shade pixels at curve transitions. Cluster size = 1 to 3 pixels. NEVER use single AA pixels - they read as noise.
anti_aliasing_clusters.minimum_cluster: 2 pixels. A cluster of 1 looks like a mistake.
anti_aliasing_clusters.max_cluster_in_outline: 3 pixels. Beyond 3 you're just adding a new line shade, not AA.
anti_aliasing_clusters.AA_color_rule: AA color = exact midpoint between the two colors being blended (use a real palette intermediate, not a different shade).

clustering_features.rule: Group small features (chips, scratches, weathering) instead of scattering them. The eye reads clusters as INTENTION; scatter reads as NOISE.
clustering_features.tile_advice: For a 16x16 tile, 1-2 cluster zones rather than 8 scattered pixels.

edge_bevels.rule: Tiles with explicit edge bevels (lighter top-left, darker bottom-right) tile poorly because the bevel becomes visible at every grid intersection.
fix_options: Skip edge bevel entirely; bevel only at object boundaries
Use 3-4 random rotation variants to break the grid
Move bevels INSIDE the tile (per-feature) rather than at the tile edge

rule_of_thirds_per_tile.rule: Place key features (knot, chip, weathering) at rule-of-thirds points within a tile (1/3, 2/3 marks). Avoids mid-tile boredom and avoids the seam.
rule_of_thirds_per_tile.source: General composition rule applied to tiles

sub_pixel_animation.rule: For low-res sprites (16x16), motion under 1 pixel doesn't exist. Animate by SHIFTING the whole sprite or by REDESIGNING individual frames - don't try to interpolate sub-pixel.
sub_pixel_animation.implication: Walk cycle = 4 distinct redesigned poses, not 4 lerp-interpolated frames.

outline_styles.selout: Selective outline - vary outline darkness based on lit/shadowed sides. Lit side = mid-tone outline; shadow side = full-dark outline.
outline_styles.no_outline: Skip outline entirely; rely on shape/silhouette and inner shading. Modern indie standard (Hyper Light Drifter style).
outline_styles.full_outline: Solid 1px outline same color all around. Cartoon/retro look.
outline_styles.1bit: Outline only, no fill. Game Boy / 1-bit aesthetic.
outline_styles.default_recommendation: selout for characters at 16-32px; no_outline for 64+; full_outline for retro/cute.

process_order.rule: For tiles: silhouette -> base color -> shadow -> highlight -> details/weathering -> AA. Never paint details first.
process_order.rule_for_chars: thumbnail/silhouette -> outline -> base color -> shadow -> highlight -> rim light -> AA -> overlay (if any).

palette_constraints.rule: Constraint breeds creativity. Pick a palette and stick to it - no 'just one more color' for the special FX.
palette_constraints.minimum_per_object: 3 shades minimum for shape readability (base + shadow + highlight)
palette_constraints.comfort_zone: 16 colors is enough for a full game. 32 is luxury. 64+ requires discipline to avoid mush.

lighting_direction.rule: Pick ONE light direction for the whole scene and respect it. Default: top-left (NW), 45 degrees down. Light hits NW edges, shadow falls to SE.
lighting_direction.exception: Rim light from opposite direction allowed (1-2 px) for separation against dark backgrounds.

quick_fix_checklist: Any single jutting pixel? Continue the run or remove it.
Any AA cluster of size 1? Grow to 2 or remove.
Banding visible? Vary thickness or insert a 3rd shade.
Mid-tones desaturated enough? If they look muddy, they're not.
Light direction consistent across the asset?
Features clustered or scattered? Cluster wins.

common_mistakes.uniform_outline: Outline same darkness all around -> object looks flat. Use selout.
common_mistakes.pure_black_shadow: Pure #000000 reads as a hole. Use a deeply-saturated dark hue from the palette instead.
common_mistakes.noise_dither: Random scatter dither for shading -> reads as static. Use ordered patterns or hand-clustered AA.
common_mistakes.anti_alias_to_background: AA-ing to a transparent background hardcodes the BG color. Always AA on the BG color you'll actually ship; better yet, skip AA on the silhouette.
